channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California Assembly approves $50M to defend immigrants and protect state policies from Trump plans
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California's Democratic-dominated Assembly endorsed up to $50 million in funding Monday to defend the state's progressive policies against challenges by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, KRON4

item.title: Some US businesses close in a 'day without immigrants.' But many say they can't lose income
item.description: Several businesses from day cares to grocery stores and hair salons closed Monday across the U.S. in a loosely organized day of protest against President Donald Trump's immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: County passes resolution calling for 'dignified treatment' along border
item.description: UPDATE: El Paso County Commissioners Court unanimously passed a resolution on Monday, Feb. 3 on immigration, calling for "dignified treatment of all." EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- El Paso County Commissioners Court is scheduled to discuss and possibly approve a resolution at its meeting on Monday, Feb.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Texas National Guard can now arrest and detain people who illegally enter the US
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas National Guard soldiers can arrest and detain people for entering the U.S. illegally from Mexico under an agreement with the Trump administration that expands the military's role in immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, WGN 9

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indianapolis man used fake deeds to sell 5 homes he didn't own
item.description: In addition to his 10-year federal prison sentence, James Henley, 35, was ordered to pay nearly $2 million in restitution.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly a month after devastating Southern California wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Pacific Coast Highway reopened Monday, nearly a month after it was closed when flames from the deadly Palisades Fire ripped through hillside Los Angeles neighborhoods, destroying properties all the way down to the beach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street falls following Trump's tariffs, but not as badly as feared in the morning
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The threat of a punishing trade war sent Wall Street on a roller coaster Monday. After initially falling sharply on worries about President Donald Trump’s tariffs, U.S. stocks pared their losses after Mexico said it had negotiated a one-month reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officer shoots suspect carrying rifle toward Memphis elementary school
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A man is in critical condition after an officer-involved shooting near an elementary school in the Vollintine-Evergreen area Monday morning. At around 10:45 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The lawsuit states Patty Day fractured her tooth, injured her arm and shoulder, and endured other significant injuries before the officers handcuffed her.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries vows to use government shutdown fight to combat Trump orders
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Monday that Democrats will use the coming battle over government funding as a vehicle to block President Trump’s early efforts to gut federal programs — a warning that raises the chances of a shutdown in the middle of next month.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chappell Roan wins best new artist Grammy Award
item.description: Chappell Roan nudged aside Sabrina Carpenter to win the Grammy for best new artist Sunday, capping a remarkably explosive rise to fame for an artist dropped by her last record label.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor says 5 injured in fatal Philadelphia plane crash remain hospitalized, 3 in critical condition
item.description: Three people injured on the ground when a jet crashed in a busy Philadelphia neighborhood, killing seven people, remain in critical condition, Mayor Cherelle Parker said Sunday. Parker said 22 people were injured and five of them remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB releases video of responders searching through eerie site of fatal Philadelphia Learjet crash
item.description: The NTSB published video showing officials at the site of the fatal Jan. 31 plane crash in Philadelphia, which killed at least seven people and injured 22 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luka Doncic heads to the Lakers and Anthony Davis goes to the Mavs in blockbuster trade
item.description: The trade was put together in secret, with only a precious few people knowing what was happening. Luka Doncic didn't know. Anthony Davis didn't know. Their coaches didn't know. Their teammates didn't know. And then it happened — an absolute blockbuster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed. Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow
item.description: PUNXSUTAWNEY, Pa. (WTAJ) — Each year Punxsutawney Phil predicts whether there will be six more weeks of winter or early spring when he emerges from his home in Gobbler's Knob on Groundhog Day. It's official! Punxsutawney Phil saw his shadow, meaning a prediction of six more weeks of winter.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he ordered precision military strike against senior ISIS planner
item.description: President Trump said he ordered precision air strikes on Saturday morning targeting a "senior ISIS Attack planner" and others the unnamed individual recruited and led in Somalia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow
item.description: PUNXSUTAWNEY, Pa. (WTAJ) — Each year Punxsutawney Phil predicts whether there will be six more weeks of winter or early spring when he emerges from his home in Gobbler's Knob on Groundhog Day. It's official! Punxsutawney Phil saw his shadow, meaning a prediction of six more weeks of winter.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, Fox 5

item.title: Beneath the surface, Groundhog Day's deep roots predate Punxsutawney Phil
item.description: Groundhog Day predictions about the coming of spring put the spotlight on Punxsutawney Phil every Feb. 2, but celebrating the midpoint between the shortest and darkest day of the year and the spring equinox has ancient roots in European agricultural life.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina put a third inmate to death in four months Friday as it goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr. was executed at 6:27 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Soldiers in deadly helicopter crash with DC plane identified
item.description: The U.S. Army has identified two of the three soldiers who were killed Wednesday night when their helicopter crashed into an airplane in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: McAllen, Texas (AP) — A federal judge extended a court agreement on Thursday ensuring safe and sanitary conditions for migrant children in federal custody a day after U.S. Customs and Border Protection was set to begin self-monitoring.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
